name: Long Point Tip
notes: Rocky tip creates a current seam that holds fish year-round. Early morning shallow bite (8-14ft) on the rocky edges is excellent May-September. Push deeper mid-day. Crayfish and goby patterns both work.

name: Long Point Inner Bay (Pottahawk Pt)
notes: Prime spawn flat — peaks early-mid June. Sheltered from NW wind. Early morning shallow bite is exceptional May-August in 4-10ft over rock/gravel. Drift live bait between Bait Island and Pottahawk. Crayfish patterns dominate here.

name: Bluff Bar (Long Point Offshore)
notes: Post-spawn holding area for bass leaving Long Point Inner Bay. Deep-summer structure. Top of the reef comes into 16-20ft — fish the edges early morning. Drop-shot goby or tube jigs. Key July-October.

name: Port Ryerse / Normandale Offshore
notes: First offshore staging area after post-spawn dispersal from Long Point Inner Bay. Rock humps in 20-35ft. Fish the top of humps in 15-20ft early morning. Drop-shot goby plastics or tube jigs July-October.

name: Turkey Point Reef
notes: Resident bass with reliable rocky structure. Protected from west wind — good in SW blow. Shallow rocky edges at 6-12ft fire early morning all summer. Multiple humps at 12-22ft for mid-day.

name: Wooley's Point / Nanticoke Offshore
notes: Prime mid-summer through fall. Former Nanticoke thermal discharge historically concentrates bait in shoulder seasons. Work the tops of humps in 15-22ft early morning. October fish stack here before winter. Best Aug-Oct.

name: Port Dover Offshore Reefs
notes: Rocky/rubble bottom 1-3 miles offshore in 12-30ft. Early morning on the shallower humps (12-18ft) is productive all summer. Convenient Port Dover launch. Tube jigs and drop-shot year round.

name: Grand River Mouth (Port Maitland)
notes: Grand River plume warms early in spring — top pre-spawn spot. Rocky substrate near mouth holds fish all season. Early morning shallow bite in 5-12ft is consistent summer through fall. Current creates feeding lanes — crayfish patterns work well near the mouth.

name: Mohawk Island Shoals
notes: Island creates multiple current breaks and wind-shadow zones. Rocky perimeter in 6-22ft. Shallow early morning bite (6-12ft) is reliable May-September on the current-washed edges. One of the most consistent multi-season spots on the Ontario shore.

name: Port Colborne / Welland Canal Mouth
notes: Canal mouth current concentrates baitfish year-round creating a consistent feeding lane. Early morning 6-12ft bite is very productive May-October. Gravelly Bay regulated zone east of breakwall — check Ontario regs. Diving crankbaits excel in current.

name: Sherkston Shoals
notes: Clean rocky shoal between Port Colborne and Crystal Beach. Early morning 6-12ft bite active all summer. Eastern basin clarity means fish spook easier in sun — first light window is critical here. Anchor or drift diving crankbaits over the flats.

name: Crystal Beach Offshore Break
notes: World-class smallmouth. The 18-22ft break is the mid-day money zone, but the 10-16ft rocky edge fires hard early morning all summer. August and October are the twin peaks. Crystal clear water — drop-shot with finesse goby plastics, lighter fluorocarbon. Best bite is first 2 hours of daylight.

name: Waverly Shoal (Fort Erie)
notes: Series of current-washed rocky humps near the Niagara outflow. Current keeps fish active and shallower than other eastern basin spots — 8-14ft early morning bite is reliable all summer. Reef tops come into 8-12ft. Near-Niagara current influence is a big advantage. Best Aug-Oct.

name: Upper Niagara River (Canadian Side)
notes: Current-driven river environment keeps fish active and shallower than lake spots — 8-14ft is productive morning to evening all summer. Current scours the rocky bottom creating goby habitat. June through November is exceptional. Check Canadian/Ontario regulations.
